snippet.title: iPhone 17 Pro: This Changes Everything
snippet.description:
I wanted to see how far the iPhone 17 Pro’s camera could really go — So instead of shooting perfect studio scenes, I took it into the real world: harsh headlights, warm street lights, messy shadows, and real skin tones.

If you’re trying to get the most cinematic video possible out of the iPhone 17 Pro, here's the tips and tricks I used that made me put down my cinema camera.





📸 iPhone 17 Pro Cinematic Video Settings

• Best settings for low-light video
• LOG vs standard color profiles
• HDR vs SDR workflow tips
• Optical vs digital stabilization choices
• ISO, shutter speed & exposure strategy
• Noise control + color profile tweaks
• Lens choices & framing approach

These settings will help you get cleaner night footage, more accurate skin tones, and a more cinematic look overall.

👑 Master Mode on OPPO (great for learning exposure/WB)

snippet.title: How to Remove Gmail from Mail App on iPhone 17 (Pro, Max) (2 Methods)
snippet.description:
Here's how to remove Gmail from the Mail app on iPhone, remove it completely, or hide it from the Mail app. 

Delete Gmail Account:- https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=10GYrszsulU

00:00 intro
00:08 Remove From Mail App
00:37 Hide From Mail App

Here are two ways to remove a Gmail account from the Mail app: either remove it completely or just hide it from the Mail app.

First, open the Settings app on your iPhone, scroll down, and find the Mail app.

Under Mail Accounts, choose the account you wanna remove completely from the Mail app and stop syncing with Mail, Contacts, Calendars, and Notes.

Tap Delete Account, then confirm by tapping Delete from My iPhone. Your Gmail account will stay safe, and you can still use it on other devices or on the web. So, no worries. To delete a Gmail account completely, check out my separate tutorial for that.

If you only wanna hide it from the device, just turn off all the toggles to hide it from the related apps. For example, if you wanna hide it from the Mail app, disable the Mail toggle, then go back to the Mail app.

Under Mailboxes, the Gmail account won’t show up anymore, but you can still use Contacts and other features linked to it on your iPhone.

snippet.title: Smartish Gripzilla iPhone 17  Review – Big, Bulky, and Weirdly Weak
snippet.description:
This is the Smartish Gripzilla is a case that’s been around for years and has always looked like it could survive a war. It’s massive, textured, and screams “tough.” But when I put it through my 30-drop test, the results were… not what I expected. Turns out, all that bulk doesn’t help your iPhone much.

Get your own Smartish® iPhone 17 Pro Max MagSafe Case - Gripzilla Protective Magnetic Case [Rugged + Tough] Heavy Duty Grip - Drop Tested Military Grade Protection by using this link: https://mreh.ca/4qQg3Ba
*This is an Amazon affiliate link that will bring you to the right Amazon store for your location

#ReviewerNotInfluencer #Reviews #iPhone

In this video:
0:00 Intro
0:34 Design
0:54 Handling & Usability
1:51 Protection Features
2:21 Drop Protection
3:54 Should You Get It?

Smartish® iPhone 17 Pro Max MagSafe Case - Gripzilla Protective Magnetic Case [Rugged + Tough] Heavy Duty Grip - Drop Tested Military Grade Protection Review

-the overall design hasn't really changed over the years
-doesn't seem as bulky as previous years
-lots of different colors to choose from
-oversized corners

-one of the grippiest cases I've tested...ever
-the finish of the case is borderline too abrasive
-but if you've got butter fingers, you're going to love it
-increase the weight of your iPhone by 58g
-increases the bulk of your iPhone by 73%
-I've been reviewing this case for 6+ years 
-I see bigger corners and just assume it's going to be tougher
-on average, at the corners, my iPhone felt 12.8g's
-that's 25% higher than average
-now the big corners does a good job of dulling how fast the case rotates after the initial bounce
-so the rate at which the case settles is 25% better than average
-BUT the overall force going into the iPhone is still 10% higher because of the massive initial hit.
-so the massive corners have more of a spring effect than a shock absorbing effect.
-i'm reviewing the Gripmunk next and this tiny case is technically better than this beefy one

-now adding insult to injury
-this case didn't pass my face first drop test
-the top of the case isn't reinforced
-so it's a bit squishy, not as bad because of the thickness
-between that and the low clearance, a iPhone with a screen protector
-is going to eat table every single time

-for the camera lump
-the initial impact was 70% higher than average at 8.1N
-the settling rate was 41% worse
-the overall force was 9% higher

-basically, wrapping your iPhone up in a ton of TPU isn't going to do it any favors.
-that's cool to see
-big TPU case
-corners are 3.8mm thick
-the back is 2.7mm
-2.4mm is the average
-BUT it's not stiff at all
-at 8mm, the back pushes back at 13N
-that's 55% worse than average
-back is quite slick
-almost runs like an air hockey puck

-takes 11.2N to pull 11.9N to push
-21% stronger than the average case
-buttons are integrated
-so damn squishy
-camera control button is covered
-smartish smoothed out the edge around the button
-so the action along the button is smooth 
-the port cutouts on Smartish cases are pretty great
-really comfortable and large
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

snippet.title: iPhone Air  Review - Beautifully FAILED
snippet.description:
The iPhone Air — Apple’s most beautiful yet impractical iPhone ever 💎.
After 40 days of real-world testing, here’s my complete review of Apple’s thinnest and lightest iPhone.
I’ve used it as my primary phone for the last 7 days, and trust me — this thing looks like jewelry but behaves like a puzzle piece of tech art.

In this video, we’ll dive deep into every detail that matters in real life —
from design & build quality, display & multimedia, A19 Pro performance, battery drain, camera quality, and software experience,
to how the iPhone Air compares to iPhone 17 and iPhone 17 Pro Max in 2025.

🧩 Key Takeaways

✅ Design: Ultra-thin 5.6 mm titanium frame — feels like holding art
✅ Display: Gorgeous 120 Hz OLED panel with 3000 nits brightness
✅ Performance: Flagship-level A19 Pro chip, lightning-fast multitasking
✅ Camera: Excellent 48 MP shots but lacks ultra-wide flexibility
✅ Battery: 7 hours + SOT, decent charging speeds

Timestamps:-
00:00 Intro
02:46 Build & Design Quality
05:05 Display
07:04 Perfomance & Thermals
09:29 Battery & Charging
11:07 Camera
12:27 Software
12:56 Network & Security & Haptics
14:47 Cons
15:35 Verdict


If you love premium design, minimalism, and exclusivity, you’ll adore the iPhone Air.
If you’re a power user, gamer, or creator, you might still prefer the iPhone 17 Pro.
